response:
Let’s check if \( 43921 \) is prime.

First, test small primes:  
- Not divisible by 2 (odd).  
- Sum of digits \( 4+3+9+2+1 = 19 \), not divisible by 3.  
- Doesn’t end in 0 or 5, so not divisible by 5.  
- Check 7: \( 43921 \div 7 \approx 6274.428... \), not integer.  
- Check 11: alternating sum \( 4 - 3 + 9 - 2 + 1 = 9 \), not divisible by 11.  
- Check 13: \( 13 \times 3378 = 43914 \), remainder 7, so no.  
- Check 17: \( 17 \times 2583 = 43911 \), remainder 10, so no.  
- Check 19: \( 19 \times 2311 = 43909 \), remainder 12, so no.  
- Check 23: \( 23 \times 1909 = 43907 \), remainder 14, so no.  
- Check 29: \( 29 \times 1514 = 43906 \), remainder 15, so no.  
- Check 31: \( 31 \times 1416 = 43896 \), remainder 25, so no.  
- Check 37: \( 37 \times 1187 = 43919 \), remainder 2, so no.  
- Check 41: \( 41 \times 1071 = 43911 \), remainder 10, so no.  
- Check 43: \( 43 \times 1021 = 43903 \), remainder 18, so no.  
- Check 47: \( 47 \times 934 = 43898 \), remainder 23, so no.  
- Check 53: \( 53 \times 828 = 43884 \), remainder 37, so no.  
- Check 59: \( 59 \times 744 = 43896 \), remainder 25, so no.  
- Check 61: \( 61 \times 720 = 43920 \), remainder 1, so no.  
- Check 67: \( 67 \times 655 = 43885 \), remainder 36, so no.  
- Check 71: \( 71 \times 618 = 43878 \), remainder 43, so no.  
- Check 73: \( 73 \times 601 = 43873 \), remainder 48, so no.  
- Check 79: \( 79 \times 556 = 43924 \), too high, so not divisible.  
- Check 83: \( 83 \times 529 = 43907 \), remainder 14, so no.  
- Check 89: \( 89 \times 493 = 43877 \), remainder 44, so no.  
- Check 97: \( 97 \times 452 = 43844 \), remainder 77, so no.  

We need to check up to \( \sqrt{43921} \approx 209.6 \).  

Let’s try a known prime test shortcut:  
Check 101: \( 101 \times 435 = 43935 \) (too high), \( 101 \times 434 = 43834 \), remainder 87.  
Check 103: \( 103 \times 426 = 43878 \), remainder 43.  
Check 107: \( 107 \times 410 = 43870 \), remainder 51.  
Check 109: \( 109 \times 403 = 43927 \) (too high), \( 109 \times 402 = 43818 \), remainder 103.  
Check 113: \( 113 \times 388 = 43844 \), remainder 77.  
Check 127: \( 127 \times 345 = 43815 \), remainder 106.  
Check 131: \( 131 \times 335 = 43885 \), remainder 36.  
Check 137: \( 137 \times 320 = 43840 \), remainder 81.  
Check 139: \( 139 \times 316 = 43924 \) (too high), \( 139 \times 315 = 43785 \), remainder 136.  
Check 149: \( 149 \times 294 = 43806 \), remainder 115.  
Check 151: \( 151 \times 290 = 43790 \), remainder 131.  
Check 157: \( 157 \times 279 = 43803 \), remainder 118.  
Check 163: \( 163 \times 269 = 43847 \), remainder 74.  
Check 167: \( 167 \times 263 = 43921 \) exactly.  

Indeed:  
\[
167 \times 263 = 43921
\]
Both 167 and 263 are primes, so \( 43921 \) is composite.

**Answer:** no
